user.title: Connect to Sunbank
user.description: Sunbank watches your solar setup and tells you how your power's doing — live, and with a heads-up before the battery runs low. Enter your Sunbank server's address and this source's Push key from the Sunbank app to link them up.

data_description.base_url: Where your Sunbank is running, for example http://192.168.1.50:8137
data_description.api_key: Open Sunbank → Integrations → Home Assistant integration, then copy that card's Push key. Do not use the Account key.
data_description.site: Leave as-is unless you run more than one Sunbank.
data_description.indoor_temp: Pick your indoor thermometer if you'd like Sunbank to show inside temperature.
data_description.outdoor_temp: Pick your outdoor thermometer if you'd like Sunbank to show outside temperature.

reauth_confirm.title: Sunbank needs your key again
reauth_confirm.description: Sunbank ({server}) stopped accepting the current Push key — it was probably changed or revoked. Open Sunbank → Integrations, copy this Home Assistant source's Push key, and paste it below to reconnect.

error.cannot_connect: Couldn't reach Sunbank at that address. Check the server is running and the address is right.
error.invalid_auth: Sunbank didn't accept that key. Copy it again from the Home Assistant integration card in Sunbank → Integrations.
error.not_push_key: That is the Account key. Use the Home Assistant integration card's Push key instead.
